perturbation.original_text: For a period of four years from the date of this Agreement (the "Restricted Period"), Seller Parties shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in The Company's Business (as hereinafter defined) or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, own an interest in, manage, operate, join, control, lend money or render financial or other assistance to or participate in, as a partner, stockholder, consultant or otherwise, any Person that competes with The Company's Business as it is conducted as of the Closing Date.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of four years from the date of this Agreement (the "Restricted Period"), Seller Parties may, directly or indirectly, engage in The Company's Business (as hereinafter defined) or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, own an interest in, manage, operate, join, control, lend money or render financial or other assistance to or participate in, as a partner, stockholder, consultant or otherwise, any Person that competes with The Company's Business as it is conducted as of the Closing Date.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not' to 'may' directly contradicts the intention of the non-competition clause. The purpose of this clause is to prevent the Seller Parties from competing with the Buyer Parties for a specified period. Changing 'shall not' to 'may' introduces uncertainty and contradiction because it makes it unclear whether the seller parties are restricted from competing with the company or not. This affects Section 13 (Remedies), where the remedy for breach is injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller Parties recognize that money damages alone would not adequately compensate Buyer Parties and its Affiliates in the event of breach by Seller Parties of this Agreement, and Seller Parties therefore agree that, in addition to all other remedies available to Buyer Parties and its Affiliates, at law, in equity or otherwise, Buyer Parties and its Affiliates shall be entitled to injunctive relief for the enforcement hereof. All rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and are in addition to and not exclusive of any other rights and remedies available, at law, in equity, by agreement or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Seller Parties 'shall not' engage in The Company's Business for four years. The changed text modifies this to 'may' engage in The Company's Business. This directly contradicts the original intention of the non-compete clause. The contradicted text states that 'money damages alone would not adequately compensate Buyer Parties' and that 'Buyer Parties... shall be entitled to injunctive relief for the enforcement hereof.' However, if the Seller Parties 'may' engage in the business, it is unclear if there is a breach, rendering the 'injunctive relief' unenforceable. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During the Restricted Period and at all times thereafter, Seller Parties will keep and hold all Confidential Information (as hereinafter defined) in strict confidence, and will not use or disclose in any way any of such Confidential Information without the prior express written consent of the Buyer Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Restricted Period and at all times thereafter, Seller Parties will keep and hold all Confidential Information (as hereinafter defined) with reasonable effort, and may use or disclose in some ways any of such Confidential Information with the prior express written consent of the Buyer Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original section 2, states that all Confidential Information will be kept and held in 'strict confidence', with the perturbation, it modifies the term 'strict' to 'reasonable effort' and added that the seller parties 'may use or disclose in some ways any of such Confidential Information', which is in direct contradiction with the original term that said that seller parties would not use or disclose in any way any confidential information. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the protection of confidential information. This affects Section 13 (Remedies), where the remedy for breach is injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller Parties recognize that money damages alone would not adequately compensate Buyer Parties and its Affiliates in the event of breach by Seller Parties of this Agreement, and Seller Parties therefore agree that, in addition to all other remedies available to Buyer Parties and its Affiliates, at law, in equity or otherwise, Buyer Parties and its Affiliates shall be entitled to injunctive relief for the enforcement hereof. All rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and are in addition to and not exclusive of any other rights and remedies available, at law, in equity, by agreement or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Seller Parties would keep Confidential Information in 'strict confidence' and 'will not use or disclose in any way any of such Confidential Information'. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'reasonable effort' and allows the Seller Parties to 'use or disclose in some ways any of such Confidential Information'. This directly contradicts the original promise of strict confidentiality and complete non-disclosure. The Contradicted Text (Section 13) relies on the assumption that Seller Parties have a strong obligation of confidentiality, entitling Buyer Parties to injunctive relief in the event of a breach. By weakening the confidentiality obligation, the changed text undermines the basis for injunctive relief, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction based on the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: During the Restricted Period, Seller Parties agree that they will not in any way, directly or indirectly, for the purpose of conducting or engaging in any business that competes with The Company Business, call upon, solicit, advise or otherwise do, or attempt to do, business with any customers of the Company engaged prior to the Closing Date (provided that Simtek shall have the right to do business with any of its current or former customers), or interfere with or attempt to interfere with any officers, employees or consultants of the Buyer Parties, induce or attempt to induce any of them to leave the employ of the Buyer Parties or violate the terms of their Contracts, or any employment arrangements, with Buyer Parties, or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, solicit to hire, hire or employ, whether as an employee, director, contractor, consultant or otherwise any officers, employees or consultants of the Buyer Parties, unless such Person's employment is terminated by the Seller Parties or its Affiliate; provided, however, that this Section 3 shall not prohibit general solicitations of or advertisements for employment by the Seller Parties that are not generally directed at any officers, employees, representatives or agents of the Buyer Parties, so long as the Seller Parties do not hire any such Person who responds to any such general solicitation or advertisement.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Restricted Period, Seller Parties agree that they may in any way, directly or indirectly, for any purpose, call upon, solicit, advise or otherwise do, or attempt to do, business with any customers of the Company engaged prior to the Closing Date (provided that Simtek shall have the right to do business with any of its current or former customers), or interfere with or attempt to interfere with any officers, employees or consultants of the Buyer Parties, induce or attempt to induce any of them to leave the employ of the Buyer Parties or violate the terms of their Contracts, or any employment arrangements, with Buyer Parties, or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, solicit to hire, hire or employ, whether as an employee, director, contractor, consultant or otherwise any officers, employees or consultants of the Buyer Parties, unless such Person's employment is terminated by the Seller Parties or its Affiliate; provided, however, that this Section 3 shall not prohibit general solicitations of or advertisements for employment by the Seller Parties that are not generally directed at any officers, employees, representatives or agents of the Buyer Parties, so long as the Seller Parties do not hire any such Person who responds to any such general solicitation or advertisement.
perturbation.explanation: In section 3, changing 'will not' to 'may' in 'Seller Parties agree that they will not in any way' contradicts the clause's intention to restrict solicitation and interference. It introduces uncertainty about the seller's obligations regarding non-solicitation and interference with the buyer's business relationships. Also, removing 'for the purpose of conducting or engaging in any business that competes with The Company Business' and replacing it with 'for any purpose', introduces a bigger scope for the seller parties to do business with any customers, since there is no longer a clause limiting that comercial relation to not be for competing with the company. This affects Section 13 (Remedies), where the remedy for breach is injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller Parties recognize that money damages alone would not adequately compensate Buyer Parties and its Affiliates in the event of breach by Seller Parties of this Agreement, and Seller Parties therefore agree that, in addition to all other remedies available to Buyer Parties and its Affiliates, at law, in equity or otherwise, Buyer Parties and its Affiliates shall be entitled to injunctive relief for the enforcement hereof. All rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and are in addition to and not exclusive of any other rights and remedies available, at law, in equity, by agreement or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'will not' with 'may', directly contradicting the original intent to restrict solicitation. Section 13 (Remedies) states that the seller parties recognize that damages would not adequately compensate the buyer parties in the event of a breach. By changing 'will not' to 'may', the enforceability of the non-solicitation agreement is significantly weakened, contradicting section 13's assumption that a breach would warrant injunctive relief. Removing 'for the purpose of conducting or engaging in any business that competes with The Company Business' and replacing it with 'for any purpose', introduces a bigger scope for the seller parties to do business with any customers, which creates a strong contradiction in Section 13 because the breach would become much more difficult to prove or enforce the remedies for.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended, modified or supplemented by the parties hereto in any manner, except by an instrument in writing signed by the Seller Parties and the Buyer Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended, modified or supplemented by any of the parties hereto in any manner, except by an oral agreement with the Seller Parties or the Buyer Parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'may not' to 'may' and adding 'by any of the parties' in Section 6 introduces a direct contradiction. The original clause restricts any modifications unless written and signed by both parties. The changed clause suggests that any party can unilaterally modify the agreement and, replace the term 'except by an instrument in writing signed' with 'except by an oral agreement' changing the way on how to proceed with the modifications. This creates uncertainty regarding how and if the agreement can be unilaterally modified, affecting the entire agreement. This affects Section 7 (No Waiver), where the terms and conditions of this agreement may be waived only by a written instrument signed by the party waiving compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms and conditions of this Agreement may be waived only by a written instrument signed by the party waiving compliance. The failure of any party hereto to enforce at any time any of the provisions of this Agreement shall in no way be construed to be a waiver of any such provision, nor in any way to affect the validity of this Agreement or any part hereof or the right of such party thereafter to enforce each and every such provision. No waiver of any breach of or non-compliance with this Agreement shall be held to be a waiver of any other or subsequent breach or non-compliance. The rights and remedies herein provided are cumulative and are not exclusive of any rights or remedies that any party may otherwise have at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that the agreement may NOT be amended except by a written instrument signed by both parties. The changed text states the agreement MAY be amended by any party except through an oral agreement with the Seller or Buyer parties. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the terms and conditions may be waived only by a written instrument signed by the party waiving compliance. The changed text creates a scenario where oral agreements might be permissible for amendments, contradicting the requirement of written instruments for waivers, creating a strong in-text contradiction due to structural flaws, as it changes how modifications can occur.

perturbation.original_text: The terms and conditions of this Agreement may be waived only by a written instrument signed by the party waiving compliance. The failure of any party hereto to enforce at any time any of the provisions of this Agreement shall in no way be construed to be a waiver of any such provision, nor in any way to affect the validity of this Agreement or any part hereof or the right of such party thereafter to enforce each and every such provision. No waiver of any breach of or non-compliance with this Agreement shall be held to be a waiver of any other or subsequent breach or non-compliance. The rights and remedies herein provided are cumulative and are not exclusive of any rights or remedies that any party may otherwise have at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms and conditions of this Agreement may be waived by any action or inaction by any party. The failure of any party hereto to enforce at any time any of the provisions of this Agreement shall be construed to be a waiver of such provision. No waiver of any breach of or non-compliance with this Agreement shall be held to be a waiver of any other or subsequent breach or non-compliance. The rights and remedies herein provided are cumulative and are not exclusive of any rights or remedies that any party may otherwise have at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause (Section 7) states that waivers must be in 'written instrument signed by the party waiving compliance'. Changing this to 'any action or inaction by any party' creates a direct contradiction by implying that a waiver can occur without any formal written agreement or signature and 'The failure of any party hereto to enforce at any time any of the provisions of this Agreement shall be construed to be a waiver of such provision' changes the understanding that failing to enforce a provision will be seen as a waiver. This introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts the original intent. This affects Section 11 (Entire Agreement, Assignability, etc), where states that This Agreement and the Asset Purchase Agreement and the documents and other agreements among the parties hereto and thereto as contemplated by or referred to herein or therein constitute the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersede all other prior agreements and understandings
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement and the Asset Purchase Agreement and the documents and other agreements among the parties hereto and thereto as contemplated by or referred to herein or therein constitute the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersede all other prior agreements and understandings, both written and oral, between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns. This Agreement is not intended to confer upon any person other than the parties hereto any rights or remedies hereunder, except as otherwise expressly provided herein and shall not be assignable by operation of law or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that waivers must be in 'written instrument signed by the party waiving compliance', emphasizing a formal, documented process. The changed text broadens this to 'any action or inaction by any party,' implying that a waiver can occur without any formal written agreement or signature. This contradicts Section 11's (contradicted text) assertion that the agreement and associated documents constitute the 'entire agreement,' superseding all prior agreements, both written and oral. Allowing waivers through 'any action or inaction' undermines the 'entire agreement' clause, as implied waivers, not documented in the agreement, could alter the contract's terms. This creates a direct conflict and contradiction within the document, thus the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and the Asset Purchase Agreement and the documents and other agreements among the parties hereto and thereto as contemplated by or referred to herein or therein constitute the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersede all other prior agreements and understandings, both written and oral, between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns. This Agreement is not intended to confer upon any person other than the parties hereto any rights or remedies hereunder, except as otherwise expressly provided herein and shall not be assignable by operation of law or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and the Asset Purchase Agreement and the documents and other agreements among the parties hereto and thereto as contemplated by or referred to herein or therein constitute a part of the agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersede all other prior agreements and understandings, both written and oral, between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement shall not be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns. This Agreement is intended to confer upon any person other than the parties hereto any rights or remedies hereunder, except as otherwise expressly provided herein and shall be assignable by operation of law or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: Section 11 originally declares that the mentioned agreements 'constitute the entire agreement' and 'shall be binding'. The changes replace 'entire agreement' with 'a part of the agreement', changes 'shall be binding' with 'shall not be binding' and changes 'is not intended to confer upon any person' with 'is intended to confer upon any person'. These changes creates contradiction. The original clause protects just the parts, with the perturbation it extends to others that aren't parties to the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Seller Parties have independently consulted with their counsel and after such consultation agree that the covenants set forth in this Agreement are reasonable and proper. In the event that any provision of this Agreement shall be determined to be unenforceable by reason of its extension for too great a period of time or over too large a geographic area or over too great a range of activities, it shall be interpreted to extend only over the maximum period of time, geographic area or range of activities as to which it may be enforceable. If, after application of the preceding sentence, any provision of this Agreement shall be determined to be invalid, illegal or otherwise unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, the validity, legality and enforceability of the other provisions of this Agreement shall not be affected thereby. Except as otherwise provided in this Section 12, any invalid, illegal or unenforceable provision of this Agreement shall be severable, and after any such severance, all other provisions hereof shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong contradiction. The original text states the agreement constitutes the 'entire agreement' and 'not intended to confer upon any person'. The changed text states the agreement constitutes 'a part of the agreement' and 'intended to confer upon any person'. This directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement as outlined in the original text and creates a conflict with the contradicted text, which assumes the original parties have independently consulted with their counsel and agreed to the terms, implying exclusivity. The changed text violates the original intent, breaking compatiblity.
